mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the rate environment is expansionary.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 8.5%, it signals labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off behavior. The mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward in response to labor slack.
regime_hint: labor market weakness or recession onset
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 60% of instances when UNRATE > 8.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening consumer demand and construction activity. This joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint signal) shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in logistic regression versus single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 94.0 indicates robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as strong production supports corporate earnings and capital investment cycles.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 58% of instances when INDPRO > 94.0, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth interacts with unemployment rate (UNRATE) to form a composite labor market signal. When PAYEMS grows > 0.3% month-over-month AND UNRATE remains below 9.5%, the mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days due to sustained labor demand supporting consumer spending.
regime_hint: labor market recovery or sustained expansion
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a multivariate model.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL above 225.0 combined with FEDFUNDS below 0.5% creates a regime of negative real rates. This interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice inflation risk and expect future monetary tightening.
regime_hint: stagflation risk or inflation surprise with constrained policy
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models (R² improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured on a quarterly basis above 13500 billion chained dollars indicates sustained economic expansion. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as strong GDP growth supports corporate revenue and profit expectations.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 56% of instances when GDPC1 > 13500, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) decline below 92.5 combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) contraction signals simultaneous supply and labor weakness. This interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as stagflation concerns and earnings recession fears dominate market sentiment.
regime_hint: supply shock with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a logistic regression model.
